commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer schema-contract judgment plus current-head visible proof, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff does not add dependencies, workflows, permissions, network calls, lifecycle hooks, or secret-resolution behavior; it is limited to schema metadata, UI analysis, generated hashes, tests, and changelog. (sha=a75f1f3c5c12fbdde59706accfb883eb1b170596)
commands.review_summary:
The PR switches base config JSON Schema generation to Zod input mode, pipes transform-backed `meta.lastTouchedAt` and Docker `setupCommand` schemas, updates Control UI union analysis, refreshes generated baseline hashes, adds tests, and updates the changelog.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +62, Docs +1, Generated 0. Total +72 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has transform-backed branches and default output-mode JSON Schema generation, while the analyzer rejects untyped union branches and the renderer shows the unsupported fallback. I did not run a live browser reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer schema-contract judgment plus current-head visible proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(config): resolve "Unsupported schema node" for meta.lastTouchedAt
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The Control UI shows "Unsupported schema node. Use Raw mode" for `config > Meta > Metadata > Config Last Touched At` since v2026.2.24 (PR #25491 introduced `z.number().transform(...)` inside a union for `lastTouchedAt`).
- **Why it matters:** Users cannot view or edit `meta.lastTouchedAt` through the config form — they must switch to Raw mode for a system-managed field.
- **What changed:** Added `.pipe(z.string())` after the transform so `toJSONSchema()` can represent the output type, matching the existing `DiscordIdSchema` pattern (`zod-schema.providers-core.ts:43-60`). Also fixed the same bug class in `setupCommand` (which produced `{}` for its entire schema node), and added a defense-in-depth filter in the UI's `normalizeUnion` to skip empty any-schema branches.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Other `anyOf + {}` patterns (e.g. `feishu.groupPolicy`) are out of scope — same root cause but different config areas that should be addressed in separate PRs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #267 (previously fixed typed `anyOf`/`oneOf` rendering, but did not handle empty `{}` branches)
- Related #25491 (introduced the `z.number().transform()` for `lastTouchedAt` in v2026.2.24)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `OpenClawSchema.toJSONSchema({ target: "draft-07", unrepresentable: "any" })` cannot represent `ZodEffects` (transforms). When it encounters `z.number().transform(...)` inside a union, it emits `{}` (the any-schema), producing `anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}]`. The UI's `normalizeUnion` function cannot render branches without a `type` property, so it marks the path as unsupported.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No test asserted that `lastTouchedAt`'s generated JSON Schema contained only representable branches.
- **Contributing context (if known):** PR #25491 added the transform to accept `Date.now()` numeric timestamps from agent file edits. The `.pipe(z.string())` idiom that makes transforms representable was already used for `DiscordIdSchema` but wasn't applied here.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/config/config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `OpenClawSchema.toJSONSchema()` must not produce empty `{}` branches in `anyOf` for `meta.lastTouchedAt`.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Directly asserts the generated JSON Schema structure — catches any future regression in the Zod-to-JSON-Schema pipeline for this field.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None — existing tests only validated Zod parsing, not JSON Schema generation.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `config > Meta > Metadata > Config Last Touched At` now renders as a string field instead of "Unsupported schema node."
- `config > Agents > ... > Sandbox > Docker > Setup Command` now renders as a string field instead of "Unsupported schema node."

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Zod: z.number().transform(...) → toJSONSchema() → {} (any-schema)
Union: anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}] → normalizeUnion → null → "Unsupported"

After:
Zod: z.number().transform(...).pipe(z.string()) → toJSONSchema() → {type: "string"}
Union: anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {type: "string"}] → normalizeUnion → {type: "string"} → ✓ renders
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Debian 13 (trixie) / aarch64 (Raspberry Pi 5)
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22.22.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI (web dashboard)
- Relevant config (redacted): `meta.lastTouchedAt: "2026-04-15T15:52:37.783Z"`

### Steps

1. Install OpenClaw v2026.4.14
2. Navigate to Control UI > Config > Meta > Metadata
3. Observe "Config Last Touched At" field

### Expected

- Field renders as a string input showing the ISO timestamp

### Actual

- Shows "Unsupported schema node. Use Raw mode"

## Evidence

- Failing test before: `meta.lastTouchedAt` schema contains `anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}]` — the `{}` branch has no `type` property
- Passing test after: `anyOf` branches all have `type: "string"`; new test in `config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts` asserts no empty branches
- Verified locally: `openclaw config schema | jq '.properties.meta.properties.lastTouchedAt'` now outputs `{type: "string", ...}` instead of `{anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}], ...}`

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Schema generation produces correct JSON Schema; existing coercion tests pass (string, numeric, out-of-range, non-date); new schema test passes; `setupCommand` schema now emits `{type: "string"}` instead of `{}`
- **Edge cases checked:** Out-of-range numeric timestamps still rejected; string timestamps still pass through unchanged
- **What you did not verify:** Full end-to-end UI rendering (headless Pi — no browser); CI suite (pre-existing `main` branch TS errors in `commands-subagents*` prevent full `pnpm check` locally)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** `.pipe(z.string())` changes the Zod type from `ZodUnion<[ZodString, ZodEffects<...>]>` to `ZodUnion<[ZodString, ZodPipeline<ZodEffects<...>, ZodString>]>`, which could affect TypeScript inference for callers accessing `meta.lastTouchedAt`.
  - **Mitigation:** The output type is already `string` (the transform returns `d.toISOString()`). The pipe just makes this explicit. Existing tests confirm no behavioral change.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted PR** — This fix was developed with Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6). The author understands what the code does and has verified the changes locally. Session logs available on request.

Testing level: Targeted unit tests run locally. Full CI suite has pre-existing failures on `main` in unrelated `commands-subagents*` files.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI's config form previously fell back to "Unsupported schema node — Use Raw mode" when rendering `meta.lastTouchedAt` and Docker `setupCommand`, because Zod's `toJSONSchema()` emitted `anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}]` for the `z.union([z.string(), z.number().transform(...)])` shape used at those paths. The empty `{}` branch is the unrepresentable any-schema produced by `unrepresentable: "any"` when the transform's output type isn't typed. Piping through `z.string()` after the transform makes both branches typed strings, which the UI's `normalizeUnion` can render directly.

- **Real environment tested**: Raspberry Pi 5 (`hostname: ultron`, `aarch64`), Linux `6.12.75+rpt-rpi-2712`, Node `22.22.2`. Two parallel checkouts:
  - `upstream/main` worktree at commit `01dda73e9bb` — produces the BEFORE schema state (bug present)
  - Branch `fix/config-lastTouchedAt-schema-node` at commit `19a6649c156` — produces the AFTER schema state (fix applied)
  - Rebased against current `upstream/main` (`99b17263a12`) on 2026-05-06; targeted tests pass; `pnpm config:schema:check` reports `[base-config-schema] ok`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw   # checkout
  pnpm install
  # (a) Run the targeted regression tests
  pnpm test src/config/config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts \
            ui/src/ui/config-form.browser.test.ts
  # (b) Confirm the regenerated schema matches the zod source
  pnpm config:schema:check
  # (c) Capture the live JSON-Schema output for `meta.lastTouchedAt` against
  #     the actual openclaw source (not a mock) — same call the Control UI's
  #     `normalizeUnion` would consume.
  node --import tsx -e "
    import('./src/config/zod-schema.ts').then(m => {
      const schema = m.OpenClawSchema.toJSONSchema({
        target: 'draft-07',
        unrepresentable: 'any',
      });
      const lastTouchedAt = schema.properties.meta.properties.lastTouchedAt;
      console.log(JSON.stringify(lastTouchedAt, null, 2));
      const json = JSON.stringify(lastTouchedAt);
      const hasEmptyBranch = /\"anyOf\":\\s*\\[[^\\]]*\\{\\s*\\}/.test(json);
      console.log('Empty {} branch present:', hasEmptyBranch);
    });
  "
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Targeted tests on the fix branch:
  ```
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.56s
  ```

  Schema generator confirms zod source and generated schema are in sync:
  ```
  [config/schema] possibly sensitive key found: (skills.entries.*.apiKey.*)
  [base-config-schema] ok
  ```

  Live `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch — BEFORE FIX (run against `upstream/main` at `01dda73e9bb`):
  ```json
  {
    "anyOf": [
      { "type": "string" },
      {}
    ]
  }
  Empty {} branch present: true
  ```

  Live `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch — AFTER FIX (run against `fix/config-lastTouchedAt-schema-node` at `19a6649c156`):
  ```json
  {
    "anyOf": [
      { "type": "string" },
      { "type": "string" }
    ]
  }
  Empty {} branch present: false
  ```

  Control UI screenshot showing the `meta.lastTouchedAt` field rendering as a typed string input (not the "Unsupported schema node — Use Raw mode" fallback): _[CC-ULTRON: TBD pending Eric's RDP/VNC walkthrough — flagged in the cover ferry note.]_

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - The `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch no longer contains an empty any-schema (`{}`), which is what the UI's `normalizeUnion` couldn't render. Both union branches are now typed strings.
  - The same fix applies to the Docker `setupCommand` field via the same `.pipe(z.string())` pattern; identical before/after shape.
  - `pnpm config:schema:check` reports `ok`, confirming the regenerated `schema.base.generated.ts` is consistent with the zod source.
  - The runtime coercion behavior is unchanged — strings pass through, numeric Unix timestamps still coerce to ISO strings, out-of-range numbers still reject. Only the JSON-Schema *representation* changed.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Live Control UI rendering — pending RDP/VNC capture (see cover ferry note). Schema-output proof above is the deterministic upstream layer; the UI screenshot is the user-visible layer.
  - All `unrepresentable` modes other than `"any"` — the bug is specific to the `"any"` mode the UI uses.

- **What was not tested**: Full end-to-end UI rendering of the lastTouchedAt schema field (headless Pi — no browser); CI suite (pre-existing main-branch TS errors in commands-subagents* prevent full pnpm check locally).
target.author: scientificprogrammer

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: fix(config): resolve "Unsupported schema node" for meta.lastTouchedAt
target.body:

- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

- Related #267 (previously fixed typed `anyOf`/`oneOf` rendering, but did not handle empty `{}` branches)
- Related #25491 (introduced the `z.number().transform()` for `lastTouchedAt` in v2026.2.24)

The UI's `normalizeUnion` function cannot render branches without a `type` property, so it marks the path as unsupported.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No test asserted that `lastTouchedAt`'s generated JSON Schema contained only representable branches.
- **Contributing context (if known):** PR #25491 added the transform to accept `Date.now()` numeric timestamps from agent file edits. The `.pipe(z.string())` idiom that makes transforms representable was already used for `DiscordIdSchema` but wasn't applied here.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/config/config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `OpenClawSchema.toJSONSchema()` must not produce empty `{}` branches in `anyOf` for `meta.lastTouchedAt`.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Directly asserts the generated JSON Schema structure — catches any future regression in the Zod-to-JSON-Schema pipeline for this field.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None — existing tests only validated Zod parsing, not JSON Schema generation.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `config > Meta > Metadata > Config Last Touched At` now renders as a string field instead of "Unsupported schema node."
- `config > Agents > ... > Sandbox > Docker > Setup Command` now renders as a string field instead of "Unsupported schema node."

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Zod: z.number().transform(...) → toJSONSchema() → {} (any-schema)
Union: anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}] → normalizeUnion → null → "Unsupported"

After:
Zod: z.number().transform(...).pipe(z.string()) → toJSONSchema() → {type: "string"}
Union: anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {type: "string"}] → normalizeUnion → {type: "string"} → ✓ renders
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Debian 13 (trixie) / aarch64 (Raspberry Pi 5)
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22.22.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI (web dashboard)
- Relevant config (redacted): `meta.lastTouchedAt: "2026-04-15T15:52:37.783Z"`

### Steps

1. Install OpenClaw v2026.4.14
2. Navigate to Control UI > Config > Meta > Metadata
3. Observe "Config Last Touched At" field

### Expected

- Field renders as a string input showing the ISO timestamp

### Actual

- Shows "Unsupported schema node. Use Raw mode"

## Evidence

- Failing test before: `meta.lastTouchedAt` schema contains `anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}]` — the `{}` branch has no `type` property
- Passing test after: `anyOf` branches all have `type: "string"`; new test in `config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts` asserts no empty branches
- Verified locally: `openclaw config schema | jq '.properties.meta.properties.lastTouchedAt'` now outputs `{type: "string", ...}` instead of `{anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}], ...}`

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Schema generation produces correct JSON Schema; existing coercion tests pass (string, numeric, out-of-range, non-date); new schema test passes; `setupCommand` schema now emits `{type: "string"}` instead of `{}`
- **Edge cases checked:** Out-of-range numeric timestamps still rejected; string timestamps still pass through unchanged
- **What you did not verify:** Full end-to-end UI rendering (headless Pi — no browser); CI suite (pre-existing `main` branch TS errors in `commands-subagents*` prevent full `pnpm check` locally)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** `.pipe(z.string())` changes the Zod type from `ZodUnion<[ZodString, ZodEffects<...>]>` to `ZodUnion<[ZodString, ZodPipeline<ZodEffects<...>, ZodString>]>`, which could affect TypeScript inference for callers accessing `meta.lastTouchedAt`.
  - **Mitigation:** The output type is already `string` (the transform returns `d.toISOString()`). The pipe just makes this explicit. Existing tests confirm no behavioral change.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted PR** — This fix was developed with Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6). The author understands what the code does and has verified the changes locally. Session logs available on request.

Testing level: Targeted unit tests run locally. Full CI suite has pre-existing failures on `main` in unrelated `commands-subagents*` files.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI's config form previously fell back to "Unsupported schema node — Use Raw mode" when rendering `meta.lastTouchedAt` and Docker `setupCommand`, because Zod's `toJSONSchema()` emitted `anyOf: [{type: "string"}, {}]` for the `z.union([z.string(), z.number().transform(...)])` shape used at those paths. The empty `{}` branch is the unrepresentable any-schema produced by `unrepresentable: "any"` when the transform's output type isn't typed. Piping through `z.string()` after the transform makes both branches typed strings, which the UI's `normalizeUnion` can render directly.

- **Real environment tested**: Raspberry Pi 5 (`hostname: ultron`, `aarch64`), Linux `6.12.75+rpt-rpi-2712`, Node `22.22.2`. Two parallel checkouts:
  - `upstream/main` worktree at commit `01dda73e9bb` — produces the BEFORE schema state (bug present)
  - Branch `fix/config-lastTouchedAt-schema-node` at commit `19a6649c156` — produces the AFTER schema state (fix applied)
  - Rebased against current `upstream/main` (`99b17263a12`) on 2026-05-06; targeted tests pass; `pnpm config:schema:check` reports `[base-config-schema] ok`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw   # checkout
  pnpm install
  # (a) Run the targeted regression tests
  pnpm test src/config/config.meta-timestamp-coercion.test.ts \
            ui/src/ui/config-form.browser.test.ts
  # (b) Confirm the regenerated schema matches the zod source
  pnpm config:schema:check
  # (c) Capture the live JSON-Schema output for `meta.lastTouchedAt` against
  #     the actual openclaw source (not a mock) — same call the Control UI's
  #     `normalizeUnion` would consume.
  node --import tsx -e "
    import('./src/config/zod-schema.ts').then(m => {
      const schema = m.OpenClawSchema.toJSONSchema({
        target: 'draft-07',
        unrepresentable: 'any',
      });
      const lastTouchedAt = schema.properties.meta.properties.lastTouchedAt;
      console.log(JSON.stringify(lastTouchedAt, null, 2));
      const json = JSON.stringify(lastTouchedAt);
      const hasEmptyBranch = /\"anyOf\":\\s*\\[[^\\]]*\\{\\s*\\}/.test(json);
      console.log('Empty {} branch present:', hasEmptyBranch);
    });
  "
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Targeted tests on the fix branch:
  ```
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.56s
  ```

  Schema generator confirms zod source and generated schema are in sync:
  ```
  [config/schema] possibly sensitive key found: (skills.entries.*.apiKey.*)
  [base-config-schema] ok
  ```

  Live `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch — BEFORE FIX (run against `upstream/main` at `01dda73e9bb`):
  ```json
  {
    "anyOf": [
      { "type": "string" },
      {}
    ]
  }
  Empty {} branch present: true
  ```

  Live `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch — AFTER FIX (run against `fix/config-lastTouchedAt-schema-node` at `19a6649c156`):
  ```json
  {
    "anyOf": [
      { "type": "string" },
      { "type": "string" }
    ]
  }
  Empty {} branch present: false
  ```

  Control UI screenshot showing the `meta.lastTouchedAt` field rendering as a typed string input (not the "Unsupported schema node — Use Raw mode" fallback): _[CC-ULTRON: TBD pending Eric's RDP/VNC walkthrough — flagged in the cover ferry note.]_

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - The `meta.lastTouchedAt` JSON-Schema branch no longer contains an empty any-schema (`{}`), which is what the UI's `normalizeUnion` couldn't render. Both union branches are now typed strings.
  - The same fix applies to the Docker `setupCommand` field via the same `.pipe(z.string())` pattern; identical before/after shape.
  - `pnpm config:schema:check` reports `ok`, confirming the regenerated `schema.base.generated.ts` is consistent with the zod source.
  - The runtime coercion behavior is unchanged — strings pass through, numeric Unix timestamps still coerce to ISO strings, out-of-range numbers still reject. Only the JSON-Schema *representation* changed.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Live Control UI rendering — pending RDP/VNC capture (see cover ferry note). Schema-output proof above is the deterministic upstream layer; the UI screenshot is the user-visible layer.
  - All `unrepresentable` modes other than `"any"` — the bug is specific to the `"any"` mode the UI uses.

- **What was not tested**: Full end-to-end UI rendering of the lastTouchedAt schema field (headless Pi — no browser); CI suite (pre-existing main-branch TS errors in commands-subagents* prevent full pnpm check locally).
